verdict.answer_first: B gets to the bottom line earlier: "100k records is small. Do not reach for Elasticsearch/OpenSearch"; A opens with meta-context before the recommendation.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder to one path: "Use Postgres's built-in full-text search" and "Don't add Elasticsearch/OpenSearch/Algolia/Meilisearch"; B becomes a branch survey with "Your branch (pick by DB)."
verdict.fabrication: A invents context not in the prompt: "Your global context leans heavily on Aurora PostgreSQL"; both give unverified latency claims, A "single-digit milliseconds" and B "under ~50ms."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it gives concrete migration/index/query SQL; B gives a good checklist but requires the user to choose a branch first.
